Arc Flash
Study

Enquire Now
  • click to scroll

Arc Flash Analysis Objectives

Advantages of Arc Flash Study

Arc Flash Methods of CareLabs

CareLabs' Service Benefit

For all enterprises, CareLabs offers affordable electrical arc flash investigation, analysis, and certification services. Qualified specialists will assess arc flash dangers, employee training, paperwork requirements, and PPE (Personal Protective Equipment) regulations to make sure your electrical safety program is up to date, compliant, and thorough. CareLabs is able to quickly recognize possible harmonic distortion threats, mitigate their effects, and guarantee the equipment’s safety. Arc outbursts are examined and analyzed using the program ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program). The quantity of heat and light that an arc fault emits is known as an arc burst. Something happens when a current flows across an air gap between conductors. 

Around 35,000 degrees Fahrenheit worth of heat and light are produced by the arc discharge, which ionizes the air. Arc flash analysis requires closely inspecting and examining a facility’s electrical systems to ascertain the incident energy that is accessible for each piece of electrical machinery. Arc discharge research encourages safe working conditions by specifying safety standards for certified electricians working on equipment and circuits. Additionally, there is a brief study of the circuit and its coordination along with updated electrical one-line schematics and equipment placements. 

Research Objectives for Arc Flash:

  • Identify the extent of harm a malfunctioning arc discharge’s incidence energy cause.
  • Modifying the protective equipment’s settings to lower incident energy exposure.
  • Give examples of the risks that discharge safety requirements pose.
  • Observe the nation’s unique laws and regulations.
  • Find out what kind of PPE is used at work.

An arc discharge has negative impacts on:

  • Face, eyes, and skin are severely injured by arcs.
  • Accidental inhalation of gases and hot particles can cause serious burns to the pharynx and airways.
  • Missing components and flying debris were to blame for the injuries.

Arc discharge studies are essential for reducing hazards, improving the safety of electrical workers, and strengthening the organization’s general security. Arc flash research makes it possible to assess the severity of electrical hazards and put in place the necessary safety precautions to lower the likelihood of fires and accidents. Arc flash analyses must be performed by all utilities, manufacturing, industrial, and commercial enterprises that use electrical disconnects, motor control, panel boards, and switches.   

Arc Flash Research Advantages:

  • Increased machine and human safety
  • Lower costs for legal and medical services
  • Adherence to legal and safety standards
  • Fewer infractions of the law and penalties

The following are the stages of arc discharge analysis:

  • Data gathering
  • Doing an inspection in the field
  • Creation of a system
  • Investigating arc flashes

CareLabs' Research Methodology for Arc Flash:

CareLabs provides top-notch services and is willing to satisfy any request from the engineering company, the neighborhood contractor, or the building crew.

Our labor technique consists of the following steps:

  • Data gathering
  • System simulation for the ETAP energy
  • Assessments that are rushed.
  • Synchronized overcurrent protection device
  • Study of arc discharges
  • Examining arc discharges
  • Keeping a journal.
  • Techniques for reducing the likelihood of arc discharges.
  • Large diagrams that just have one line.
  • There are fitted arc light indicators.
  • The finished document.

Benefits of Using CareLabs' Services:

  • Boost the security of electrical systems and guarantee legal compliance.
  • Integrated modules are used for thorough short circuit, overcurrent coordination, device assessment, and arc flash evaluation in addition to safety devices and bus ratings.
  • Increasing workplace safety
  • Simple ideas for improved designs
  • Increased safety margins by using arc fault limits that the user defines.
  • You will save time by using our computer-aided analysis to automatically generate arc flash labels.
  • Reduce the expense of maintenance and productivity losses.

To ensure that our professionals can be contacted in case of a regular or urgent issue, crew members are stationed in important locations of CareLabs. CareLabs quickly established itself as an ISO 9001:2008-certified business with a client that frequently gave it favorable reviews. We offer arc flash investigation and analysis services in all major cities, in addition to Istanbul, Ankara, Izmir, and Bursa. For a free estimate on arc discharge studies and analysis, get in touch with us right now.

  • Power System Study & Analysis
  • Motor Start Analysis

Harmonic Study &
Analysis

Enquire Now
  • click to scroll

Effects of Harmonic Distortion

Harmonic Analysis Workflow

Carelabs' Service Benefit

Related Services

CareLabs offers electrical installation services that include electrical safety inspections, testing, calibration, and certification, in addition to a full examination and analysis of harmonics. Experts will review harmonic risks, training materials, documentation, and personal protective equipment (PPE) to ensure that your electrical safety program is up to date, compliant, and thorough.  CareLabs can identify harmonics early and take the necessary action to reduce their impact to safeguard the apparatus. When there are harmonic electric voltages and currents, electrical systems may become less efficient. They have the power to degrade electrical quality and produce unnecessary heat. 

Harmonic voltage analysis predicts harmonic distortion and resonances mathematically. Harmonic voltage analysis calculates potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels using empirical data. Harmonic frequencies are added to the electrical system by nonlinear electrical switching components including AC variable frequency motors, DC actuators, fluorescent lights, and computers. High voltage/current harmonic levels considerably increase the risk of equipment failure. Making a rapid harmonic analysis and research is therefore essential. The current harmonic constraints for 415-volt systems are 5%, with 4% odd harmonics and 2% even harmonics.     

The Effects of Harmonic Distortion:

  • Responsible for both current flow and high voltage.
  • Due to significant voltage distortion, equipment failure and destruction
  • Because internal energy is increasing, component lifespan is decreasing, and component failure is increasing.
  • When an issue emerges, branch circuit breakers trip.
  • Measuring blunder
  • Electrical and distribution network fires and explosions
  • Generator problems
  • Reduced system power factor
  • Installation and utility expenses that are high
  • Reduced productivity
  • Driving while being careless and distracted
  • Use of stronger motors

Even though harmonic problems are exceedingly uncommon, when they do arise, they tend to lower the reliability, effectiveness, and quality of the power system. Therefore, harmonic investigation and analysis are crucial.

  • Protections against harmonic voltage and current
  • Restricted variation in frequency
  • Variability of current and voltage
  • Boost power factor analysis and maintain a value close to one.
  • Regulates the voltage’s peaks and troughs

Harmonic research and analysis will help clarify the most probable cause and effect of power quality problems by minimizing harmonic-related concerns during the design process and reducing the possibility of unexpected consequences.   

Harmonic Analysis and Research Workflow at CareLabs:

  • Indicate any possible nonlinear loads, capacitor banks, and lengthy medium-voltage cables on the electrical single-line diagram.
  • Locate the PCC or point of common coupling.
  • Determine which system buses in the building are susceptible to harmonic distortions.
  • Gather harmonics data for each nonlinear load.
  • Obtain critical information on the highest and lowest short circuit fault levels for the voltage and current harmonics of the specified PCC.
  • Use ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program) to analyze the harmonics of the electrical network.
  • Calculate the overall and individual current and voltage wave distortion values for the bus and PCC.
  • The harmonic frequency spectrum should be represented graphically.
  • If harmonic distortion levels are too high, a suitable mitigation strategy is suggested and implemented.
  • After the mitigation technique has been implemented, run the study again.

Benefits of Harmonic Analysis Service Includes:

In the second phase, higher ratings are used if background harmonics have already been measured.

  • Stage 2 permits changing the converter size in accordance with the fault level and converter type if the background is less than 25%.
  • If the background is between 25% and 75%, the lower half of the converter grade is appropriate.
  • We study the harmonics of the upper background.

CareLabs employs workers stationed in several locations to make sure that personnel is readily available in the event of a routine or urgent emergency. CareLabs has swiftly made a name for itself as an ISO 9001:2008-accredited company with committed customers who have provided positive comments. In a few significant Turkish cities, including Istanbul, Ankara, Izmir, and Bursa, we do harmonic research and analysis. To set up your harmonic analysis and study in Turkish or to get a price, get in contact with us right now!

SERVICES

Toggle menu

Motor Start
Analysis

Enquire Now
  • click to scroll

Why it is Necessary?

What is Motor Start Analysis?

Motor Start Advantages?

Related Services

The exclusive Motor start research and analysis solution from CareLabs may be advantageous to Turkish businesses. The primary objective of our motor start analysis service is to evaluate any unforeseen effects of starting a large motor. Before starting an electric motor, it is essential to check for additional indicators of a faulty power supply, such as nuisance tripping, excessive nominal currents, and flickering or dimming light, in addition to the line voltage. CareLabs will send qualified personnel to your location to acquire information on the most recent and earlier motor installations. Then calculations are performed to project probable outcomes, and suggestions are offered to mitigate the effects based on the data obtained and manufacturer feedback.     

Both static and dynamic simulation techniques may be used to investigate motor starting procedures. Studies that faithfully replicate the conditions that exist when an electric motor starts to run include:

  • Modeling the measured starting impedance of the electric motor yields the load flow solution.
  • The voltage drop method is utilized for motor starting currents, while the short-circuit method is employed in pre-fault brief circuit situations.
  • If a comprehensive dynamic model of the circuit and the load-torque characteristics is given, it could be possible to evaluate the effect using the electric motor’s dynamic model. For motor starting transient stability, the standard procedure.
  • If the whole dynamic model of the electric motor is not known, simulations of transient stability may be employed.

Motor Starts Need to Be Inspected in Turkey:

Both the size and the application of electric motors in contemporary industrial systems are dramatically expanding. Starting a sizable motor that is linked to a line may cause interference with the electric motor, neighboring loads, and far-off electrically driven cars. When starting an electric motor, the terminal voltage should be at or more than 80% of the rated voltage. Due to the fact that the square of a motor’s terminal voltage is exactly proportional to torque, voltage differential affects the load torque of the motor. If the voltage falls significantly, machines in motion may approach their breaking torque and slow down or stop. Voltage changes have an impact on a wide range of objects, including electrical machinery, delicate control systems, and lighting loads. All systems supplying a continuous load require more current when the initial current is large because it decreases the terminal voltage. Incorrect undervoltage relays have also been discovered.  

When Do You Need a Motor Start Analysis?

  • Prior to or throughout the acquisition of a potent powertrain.
  • The supply transformer will break down in the absence of a generator and if the motor rating is more than 30% of the base KVA rating.
  • If the generator is the only source of power for the system and the motor rating exceeds 10% to 15% of the generator’s KVA rating, the generator has to be replaced.
  • If many motors start up at once during an industrial operation, a motor start analysis may be necessary.
  • Starting a motor with a large capacity is more difficult with smaller power sources since they frequently have a restricted capacity due to issues with voltage loss. An examination of the motor sources facilitates the understanding of the output of small generator-containing systems.

Investigating the Advantages of Motor Start:

Research on motor launches Utilizing a motor or starting method with advantageous speed-torque characteristics is advantageous. Any unexpected loads must be increased while being closely monitored to prevent going above the equipment’s torque limits. Any time the motor is started, the performance of the motor shaft may be examined. As a result, it aids in determining the machinery’s torque limitations. In tests, an electric motor is started to determine its voltage and if it can go forward.  

CareLabs offers skilled individuals to carry out motor start examinations so they can assess your electric motor further. All major Turkish cities, including Istanbul, Ankara, Izmir, and Bursa, are also served by our motor start examination services. Contact us for a quote or schedule your motor start analysis right now!

Power Quality

Evaluation Of Power Quality

Why Analyze Power Quality?

Power Quality Analysis Factors?

Related Services

CareLabs evaluates the electricity situation for Turkish companies. Our aim is to safeguard your appliances, equipment, and electrical systems from problems caused by erratic power sources. The data collection, monitoring, and instrumentation technologies of today are used by numerous businesses and an increasing number of people. The voltage fluctuation spectrum, a common aspect of distributed power, is something that nearly no electrical equipment is designed to withstand. CareLabs can assist with issues with device installation, control, protection, and power conditioning.    

The Evaluation of Power Quality Aims To:

  • The load and the electrical system’s differences are evaluated.
  • The examination of the workplace will enhance simulation methods for providing solutions for power quality.
  • Assessing the efficiency and performance of the load-bearing equipment
  • Boost the machine’s power factor and reliability.
  • Cut back on your electricity consumption.
  • To keep issues and disruptions at bay.
  • Reducing irregularities in power quality including harmonics, surges, and transients.
  • Assist in preventing PLC lockups and voltage/frequency spikes.
  • To prevent capacitor detonation, overloading of the transformer, and irritation tripping.
  • Drastically reduced utility costs
  • Increase the lifespan of the network and its parts.
  • Verify that the electrical equipment can withstand changes by doing so.

Power Quality Must Be Examined in Turkey:

All electrical networks are susceptible to supply interruptions, which occur more often when storms, accidents, or other natural disasters destroy power lines. Electricity availability varies considerably. Three distinct types of fluctuations, disturbances, and distortions can affect the characteristics of power delivered by an electrical network.

1. A frequency deviates from its usual range when it exceeds it.

2. When the voltage form is maintained constant, but the voltage amplitude is larger than usual, voltage disruptions occur.

2.1 Surges and drops in voltage that last under a minute are considered short-lived voltage fluctuations.

2.2 Long-lasting voltage fluctuations are described as persistent under- and overvoltages.

2.3 An unequal load distribution between phases causes unbalanced voltage, which dramatically increases equipment heating.

2.4 Differences in voltage between neutral and earth

3. Disturbances caused by voltage distortion, which occur when a voltage is neither sinusoidal nor uniform in shape.

3.1 Higher losses, device heating, and power inefficiency are all caused by harmonics and inter harmonics.

3.2 Brief transients that might damage switch contact surfaces, reduce system efficiency, and speed up the failure of motor drives and control.

What Factors Are Considered in a Power Quality Analysis?

The following are a few instances of the many power quality issues that necessitate various methods of identification:

  • In harmonic analysis, computer algorithms are used to identify and anticipate potential harmonic issues and provide solutions.
  • Through this research, analysis of surges and transients will be revealed.
  • The study will monitor the voltage’s value, direction, and short-term rise and decline.
  • Evaluation of Reactive Force Through this investigation, the preferred reactive power at the distribution and load extremities will be determined.
  • This concept would reduce petroleum use and energy surcharges by coordinating and monitoring captive electricity to match demand.
  • This research predicts voltage levels, losses in system branches, power flow sizes, and power factor based on the provided operational parameters.

CareLabs provides the highest caliber of service to increase the dependability and quality of your electrical system for the benefit of all its customers. In Istanbul, Ankara, Izmir, and Bursa, we primarily assess the quality of the energy. Contact us right now to set up a power quality test or to get a quotation!  

OUR

SERVICES

CareLabs

Services

  • Arc Flash Study
  • Harmonic Study & Analysis
  • Power Quality Analysis
  • Power System Study & Analysis
  • Motor Start Analysis

CareLabs is a global leader in electrical engineering services, providing innovative, reliable, and environmentally responsible solutions in multiple sectors.

Power System Study &
Analysis

Enquire Now
  • click to scroll

Why it is Necessary?

Why Analyze Power Quality?

Power Quality Analysis Factors?

Related Services

CareLabs provides a variety of electrical installation services, including electrical safety inspections, power quality studies, simulations of electrical system designs, and studies and analyses of power systems. Our electrical experts use ETAP, the most efficient power system transient analysis tool, for load flow and short circuit studies, protective device coordination studies, harmonic emission and filter design studies, transient analysis, and network stability studies. This enables us to develop and implement the finest power compensation and solutions in collaboration with our clients.  

Why Is a Power System Analysis Necessary?

Every industrial or commercial structure must have a dependable, regular, and well-maintained electrical system. Any power outage can have severe consequences, including prolonged downtime, damaged essential equipment, and greater repair costs. A frequent evaluation of your facility’s power system might vouch for its dependability, security, and efficiency in both ordinary and exceptional conditions. A power system needs to go through a thorough investigation at every stage, including design, installation, and operation. The ETAP software tool will be used in our power system studies and assessments to provide a basic understanding of how the system will operate under both normal operation and transient fault situations. It stands for a collection of distinctive engineering analytical studies that have been conducted to examine how an electric power system responds to events that occur across a variety of time periods.  

The subject of numerous inquiries has been the condition of the electrical system at your company. 

Examples of power system analysis may be used in a research paper:

  • Circuit switch
  • Coordinated security measures
  • Carrying a load
  • Dynamic and prompt assessment
  • Arc flash evaluation
  • Powerful force
  • Looking into harmonics
  • The automobile’s debut
  • Research on earthing

It aids organizations in establishing benchmarks for other plant performance indicators, such the electrical safety program. These findings suggest that dependable and secure operations could be possible.     

A Load Flow Analysis:

Load flow studies give information that may be used to calculate the voltages and currents in various network segments and to select the equipment that has the proper ratings. It is possible to do load flows using ETAP’s integrated load flow function, which provides information on the power factor, voltages, and currents of a power system.  

Short-circuit Investigations:

To understand how power system failures occur, short circuits must be investigated. It takes a lot of time to manually inspect short circuits; competent software like ETAP is far more efficient. ETAP is used to simulate system protection devices and analyze the consequences of three-phase, L-G, L-L, and L-L-G failures on distribution systems.     

Temporal Studies:  

Transient studies are used to investigate the characteristics and magnitude of transient voltages and currents caused by lightning strikes and switching processes. The results define the necessary amount of surge protection and insulation.     

Investigating Protection Coordination:

This study aims to validate the system’s security components. This verifies that they are the right scale and size for the machinery they are protecting.   

Our approach for analyzing electricity systems typically includes the following important steps:

  • Data collection techniques
  • Client consultations and data analysis
  • Putting together equipment information
  • A straightforward one-line chart
  • Modeling the ETAP power system.
  • A study of short circuits and load transfer
  • Study of collaboration in protection
  • Arc discharge evaluation
  • A summary of the research.
  • Preparing for risk mitigation and assessment
  • Project management

The Carelabs Power System Analysis Consists of the Following Elements:

  • Options for lowering energy costs and use.
  • Highlighting problematic areas in reports.
  • Recommendations for remedies
  • Information about the consumption of energy and where it is consumed.
  • We look at safety device ratings.
  • Help shield against injury and ruin.
  • Effects of transient and harmonic sound waves

Our power system analysis studies are primarily concerned with analyzing the customer’s electrical system and offering suggestions for ways to improve its productivity and supply security. Power system analysis and evaluation is one of our primary service areas in Istanbul, Ankara, Izmir, and Bursa. To schedule a power system evaluation and analysis or to get a price, get in contact with us right now!  

OUR

SERVICES

CareLabs

  • Arc Flash Study
  • Harmonic Study & Analysis
  • Power Quality Analysis
  • Power System Study & Analysis
  • Motor Start Analysis
